paper_metadata.title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct
paper_metadata.abstract: Large language models (LLMs), such as GPT-4, have shown remarkable performance in natural language processing (NLP) tasks, including challenging mathematical reasoning. However, most existing open-source models are only pre-trained on large-scale internet data and without math-related optimization. In this paper, we present WizardMath, which enhances the mathematical CoT reasoning abilities of LLMs without using external python tools, by applying our proposed Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF) method to the domain of math. Through extensive experiments on two mathematical reasoning benchmarks, namely GSM8k and MATH, we reveal the extraordinary capabilities of our model. Remarkably, WizardMath-Mistral 7B surpasses top-tier open-source LLMs by a substantial margin with higher data efficiency. Furthermore, WizardMath 70B even outperforms GPT-3.5-Turbo, Claude 2, Gemini Pro and GPT-4-early-version. Additionally, our preliminary exploration highlights the pivotal role of instruction evolution and process supervision in achieving exceptional math performance. For more details refer to https://github.com/nlpxucan/WizardLM

extraction_data.innovation: WizardMath introduces Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF), which combines Math Evol-Instruct for instruction generation and process supervision via a Process-Supervised Reward Model (PRM) and Instruction Reward Model (IRM) to enhance mathematical reasoning without external tools.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning with Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF)
extraction_data.paper_title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct

extraction_data.innovation: WizardMath introduces a Reinforced Evol-Instruct (RLEIF) framework that combines instruction evolution and process supervision using a Process Reward Model (PRM) to improve mathematical reasoning. The PRM enables precise detection of incorrect reasoning steps, outperforming ORM and majority voting strategies.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Supervised fine-tuning with Reinforcement Learning from Evol-Instruct Feedback (RLEIF)
extraction_data.research_problem: Mathematical reasoning in large language models
extraction_data.application: Mathematical problem solving, instruction refinement, reasoning enhancement
extraction_data.paper_title: WizardMath: Empowering Mathematical Reasoning for Large Language Models via Reinforced Evol-Instruct
